observations.recommendation:
1.13 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a)
Municipal Accountant and OIC - Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their
cash records at least quarterly and adjust respective records accordingly
for any identified error; and (b) Municipal Accountant to prepare and
submit BRS on a monthly basis so that errors or discrepancies on
recorded amounts would be detected immediately and proper
adjustment could be made in the books.

2.14 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to advise the
concerned municipal officials to implement the following corrective
measures: (a) ensure that the concerned Accountable Officers immediately
liquidate all their outstanding cash advances through submission of
liquidation documents and/or refund of the unexpended balances; and that
all cash advances should be liquidated as soon as the purpose/s of the said
cash advances have already been served; (b) exhaust all possible legal and
corrective remedies against former accountable officers such as sending
demand letters, and securing copy of death certificate and other supporting
documents in case of death of the accountable officers; (c) refrain/stop from
granting additional cash advances to officers and employees unless they
have liquidated their previous cash advance/s, or designate other bonded
employees with no outstanding cash advance; and (d) require full
settlement of all outstanding cash advances before clearing an official or
employee from accountabilities.

3.16 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the (a)
Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to (i) conduct a complete
physical count of all PPEs of the Municipality on or before January 31 of
each year; (ii) conclude the inventory count for CY 2024 and immediately
submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team; and (iii) reconcile the resultant
inventory report with the accounting records; and (iv) MGSO to continue
to update the Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment (ARE) for all
PPEs issued to officials and employees; (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to
complete the subsidiary ledger accounts for PPE to substantiate the
authenticity of figures presented in the financial statements; and (c) OIC-
Municipal Accountant and MGSO to consider adopting the one-time
cleansing option for PPE accounts for fair presentation of the said asset
account in the financial statements, as prescribed in COA Circular No.
2020-006.

4.7 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the
designated GSO to (a) procure insurance policy covering all insurable
assets of the Municipality; and (b) prepare and submit the Property
Insurance Form (PIF) in accordance with Item 5 of COA Circular No.
2018-002 dated May 31, 2018.

5.5 We reiterated our recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed
to instruct the OIC - Municipal Accountant to (a) recognize in the
respective fund’s books the unrecorded transactions; and (b) regularly
reconcile the balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other
Funds accounts to enhance the reliability of the said accounts’ balances.

6.5 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a)
OIC-Municipal Treasurer to (i) maintain updated RPTAR; and (ii) prepare and
submit an updated certified list of taxpayers and amount due thereof at the
beginning of the year to the Municipal Accountant; and (b) Municipal
Accountant to recognize RPT Receivables and SET Receivables based on
the said certified list of taxpayers and tax due submitted by the OIC-Municipal
Treasurer.

7.8 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a)
Municipal Human Resources Management Officer to (i) expedite the
updating of leave records of the 121 employees of the Municipality; and (ii)
furnish the Accounting Office with the computed total money value of leave
credits of all employees that will be the basis of the latter in recording in the
books of accounts; and (b) Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Budget Officer to take coordinated actions to increase revenue
collections and allocate sufficient budgetary provisions to facilitate the
recognition of expense and liability accounts related to employee benefits.

8.20 We recommended and Management agreed to (a) continue to adhere with
the provisions of RA No. 9003 or the SWM Act of 2000 on the appropriation
and implementation of its SWM Program; (b) take appropriate action to fully
utilize the donated machineries intended for the MRFs; and secure complete
documents from the donor agency as basis for recording the said equipment;
and (c) strictly implement and enforce the policies embodied in the Municipal
Ecological Solid Waste Management Code.

9.17 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the (a)
Municipal Development Council (MDC) to be more prudent and cautious
in formulating programs/projects/ activities (PPAs) under the 20% DF
with a view of ensuring the smooth implementation of projects; (b)
Municipal Engineer and other Officials concerned to efficiently supervise
and monitor the full implementation of all PPAs to further improve
performance in the delivery of completed projects within the targeted time
frame and correspondingly achieve the optimum utilization of the 20% DF
for the year; and (c) BAC Chairman to initiate the submission of required
supporting documents under RA 9184 and its IRR.

10.16 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a)
LDRRM Officer and the OIC – Municipal Accountant to comply with the
planning and reporting guidelines for LDRRMF as provided for under
NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and COA
Circular No. 2012-002; and (b) effect the corrections in the reporting of
LDRRMF and observe proper procedures in the preparation of the reports.

11.11 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the
responsible officer to (a) ensure timely submission of the adjusted GPB to
the DILG for review and endorsement, in compliance with the PCW-
DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) Nos. 2013-01 and
2016-01 (b) comply with the mandatory budget for GAD which is at least
5% of the total appropriation and lay down activities geared toward the
attainment of GAD objectives and implement GAD related activities as
required under Section 36(a) of R.A. No. 9710 or the Magna Carta of
Women; (c) monitor and evaluate GAD PPAs whether the same would
make an impact on the gender issues present within the agency pursuant to
Section 36(a) of R.A. No. 9710 or Magna Carta for Women; and (d)
integrate in the GPB and effectively implement the PPAs that eliminate or
stop gender discrimination against women and encourage women’s
empowerment.

12.22 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) convene all
responsible heads of offices and admonish them of their duties and
functions on the submission of the accounts/transaction documents and
financial reports as sanctioned by COA; (b) instruct the Municipal
Accountant and the OIC – Municipal Treasurer to observe the timeliness
in the submission of reportorial requirements of the Commission; and (c)
direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the bank reconciliation
statements and financial statements to the office of the Auditor for the
months of January to December, 2024 and periodic trial balances for July
to December, 2024.

13.8 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant and the OIC - Municipal Treasurer to (a) settle
immediately the unremitted taxes with the BIR; and (b) strictly adhere with
the timely and intact remittance of taxes withheld as required under
Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 and BIR Tax Revenue
Regulation No. 10-2008 dated March 23, 2007, and July 8, 2008,
respectively.

14.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a)
OIC –-Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to (i) strictly
adhere with the timely and intact remittance of contributions and loan
payments to the GSIS in accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 8291;
and (ii) settle immediately the unremitted balance with the GSIS; and (b)
OIC – Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to facilitate the
immediate updating of personnel records in GSIS system.

15.7 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a)
Municipal Accountant to (i) adhere with the timely and intact remittance
of employees’ contributions and loan payments to the Pag-IBIG in
accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 9679; and (ii) settle immediately
the unremitted balance with the Pag-IBIG pertaining to the year-end
balance of ₱1,399,103.47; and (b) OIC – Municipal Human Resources
Management Officer to facilitate the immediate updating of personnel
records in the Pag-IBIG system.

16.8 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a)
OIC – Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to (i) remit the
employees’ contributions fully and intact, and strictly observe compliance
to Section 18 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of the
National Health Insurance Act of 2013; (ii) reconcile the Municipality’s
records with PhilHealth; and (iii) settle immediately the unremitted
balance to PhilHealth; and (b) OIC – Municipal Human Resources
Management Officer to facilitate the immediate enrollment and updating
of personnel records in PhilHealth system.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to require the OIC –
Municipal Accountant and the OIC
– Municipal Treasurer to
(a) reconcile their cash records at
least quarterly and effect the
necessary adjustment to resolve the
noted discrepancy; and
(b) secure access to online bank
facilities and timely download the
monthly bank statements to
immediately detect any possible
difference between the bank and
treasury records and effect the
necessary reconciliation.

We reiterated our earlier recommendation that Management instruct the concerned officials, particularly the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 requiring the submission of Purchase Orders/contacts and supporting documents within five (5) days after issuance/perfection to the Office of the Auditor to ensure timely review/evaluation of the contracts and inspection of deliveries, respectively.

Considering that the subject deficiencies had been repeatedly discussed in prior years’ report,   there is a compelling reason that the recommended courses of actions and corrective measures offered should be given serious consideration by the Management.

We recommended that management:
Prioritize the conduct of the complete physical inventory as required in Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for  Local Government Units Volume 1; and
Reconcile the results of the physical inventory with the accounting and property records, and prepare and effect the necessary adjustments in order to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

Cause the immediate liquidation of the cash advances granted to LGU officials/employees and/or the withholding of salaries or any money due the officers and employees with overdue unliquidated cash advances to compel its settlement; and

liquidated or reported as soon as the purpose of the cash advance is served;

2.4 We recommended that the management:
a. Send demand letters to the recipients of Poverty Alleviation Programs with
uncollected receivable balance in the books. Furthermore, strict monitoring
of the uncollected balances of receivable accounts should be done by the
Accountant or concerned employee to facilitate its timely collection.
b. File the request for authority to write off dormant receivable accounts to the
COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 if all the conditions and
requirements provided in the circular could be complied.

4.11. We recommended the following courses of actions:
a) The immediate submission of the Reports of Actual Inventory of PPE;
b) Reconcile the result of the actual physical inventory report with the
accounting record and investigate discrepancies as required in Section
491 of GAAM Volume I;
c) Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards should be updated and properly
maintained by the GSO and Accountant/Bookkeeper, respectively, in
order to monitor the acquired assets of the municipality in compliance
with Section 43 of the NGAS Manual ; and
d) Depreciation of the acquired properties and equipment should be
properly computed and recorded in the books of the municipality to
correctly reflect the value of the assets.

and cause the approval of the annual School Board Budget for SEF by

DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998, and No. 01-
A, March 14, 2000; and
(b) stop the practice of utilizing the fund for payment of MOOE of the
DepEd Office and other expenses not related to or included in the list
of prioritized expenses chargeable against SEF.

a. require the insurance of all government properties such as motor
vehicles, machineries, permanent buildings and properties stored therein
against any insurable risk with the General Insurance Fund by the
GSIS; and

14.6 We recommended that taxes for all payments made to suppliers and
contractors be deducted and promptly remitted as required under the Section 2.81
of BIR Revenue Regulation 2-98 to prevent surcharge or interest from accruing.

We recommended that management stop the practice of using cash reserve for trust liabilities for payment of regular expenditures to avoid the incurrence of cash overdraft and to promptly remit trust funds due to other government agencies. Control disbursements based on available funds/appropriation and not on available cash balances. Trust fund account should be made available only for the specific expenditures for which it was created or received.

We recommended the following courses of action:

a. Strict compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting of cash advances and the subsequent liquidations thereof.  

b. Closely monitor the unliquidated cash advances and send demand letters to the officers and employees requiring its immediate settlements.    

c. Exert effort in locating documents   particularly on cash advances under the Special Education Fund and Trust Fund to properly establish the responsible accountable officer.
